Notice Of Discovery Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant/Cross-Defendant Side, Inc. To Attend And Produce Documents At Deposition Before Expert Deposititons; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant/Cross-Defendant Side, Inc.
Off Calendar May-11-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Fiona Walsh-Dinan's Motion To Compel Defendant/Cross-Defendant Side, Inc. To Attend And Produce Documents At Deposition Before Expert Depositions is OFF CALENDAR.

Moving Defendant has not demonstrated she timely filed or served her motion. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b) [motion must be filed and served 16 court days before hearing]; Code of Civil Procedure section 1013(a) [service by mail where place of address and place of mailing are within State of adds 5 calendar days to the period of notice]; see Code of Civil Procedure section 1010.6(a)(3)(B) [service by email adds two court days to "any period of notice"]; Code of Civil Procedure section 1013(e) [service by fax only be agreement and adds two court days to "any period of notice"].) Moving Defendant filed and served by email her motion by email on 4/22/2026, which is not 16 court days-let alone 16 court days plus two court days-before 5/11/2026, the hearing date noticed. Moving Defendant's disregard for the rules is not excused.

A party seeking to have a motion heard outside the regular noticed period must secure an order shortening time or advancing the hearing. Defendant has no such order and, therefore, its attempt to jump the line fails and its untimely motion is ordered off calendar.

Nor did Moving Defendant notice her motion for at least 15 days before the current (let alone initial) date. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2024/020(a).) The motion, thus, is untimely.

Defendant Fiona Walsh-Dinan is ordered to prepare a proposed order consistent with the above and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
